projects.title: Microbiology Lab: Microbial Culture & Aseptic Technique
projects.description: Hands-on experience in media preparation, equipment sterilization, bacterial inoculation, and controlled incubation following standard laboratory protocols.

details.overview: Conducted comprehensive microbiology laboratory procedures including NA/PDA media preparation, autoclave sterilization, aseptic bacterial inoculation using pour plate, streak plate, spread plate methods, and controlled incubation for bacterial growth. All procedures followed standardized operating protocols (SOP) with emphasis on aseptic technique and laboratory safety.
details.challenge: Maintaining sterility throughout the entire workflow is critical — any contamination from airborne particles, improper technique, or unsterilized equipment can compromise results and waste laboratory resources.
details.solution: Implemented strict aseptic workflow: flame sterilization of inoculation loops, work within Bunsen burner aseptic zone, proper labeling and timing of each step, and parallel control plates to verify sterility. Developed systematic quadrant streak plate patterns for optimal single colony isolation.
results: Successfully prepared nutrient agar (NA), potato dextrose agar (PDA), and broth media
Achieved consistent aseptic technique with zero contamination in negative control plates
Obtained isolated single colonies suitable for pure culture transfer and further analysis
Properly documented incubation parameters

gallery.caption: NA/PDA media preparation
Aseptic transfer technique using inoculation loop near Bunsen burner
Pure culture isolation on nutrient agar plate
Fungial purification — sub-culturing isolated colonies
Isolation technique (Spread Plate, Pour Plate, Streak Plate)
Spread plate method for viable colony counting

projects.title: Microbial Identification & Morphological Analysis
projects.description: Performed comprehensive macroscopic-microscopic bacterial and fungal characterization.

details.overview: Conducted bacterial and fungal identification through a systematic multi-step approach: differential Gram staining to classify cell wall structure, biochemical tests for species-level identification, slide culture technique to examine fungal colonies, and detailed macroscopic-microscopic morphological observation. All procedures followed established microbiological identification protocols.
details.challenge: Accurate bacterial and fungal identification requires precise execution of multiple sequential techniques — errors in staining timing, reagent concentration, or incubation conditions can lead to misclassification. Colony counting demands consistency and proper dilution technique to obtain countable plates (30-300 colonies).
details.solution: Followed strict timing protocol for each Gram staining step (crystal violet 60s, iodine mordant 60s, decolorizer 10-15s, safranin counterstain 45s). Performed duplicate slides and triplicate plates for result verification. Used proper serial dilution technique for colony counting and recorded all observations using standardized documentation sheets.
results: Correctly classified bacteria into Gram-positive (purple) and Gram-negative (pink) groups
Identified cell morphology: cocci, bacilli, and arrangements (chains, clusters, diplococci)
Identified fungi macroscopic characteristics: color, texture, radial furrow, growing zone, etc
Successfully correlated macroscopic colony characteristics with microscopic observations
Documented complete identification workflow with photographic evidence
techniques: Gram Staining (Crystal Violet, Iodine, Decolorizer, Safranin)
Biochemical Tests (Catalase, Starch hydrolysis test, Simmons Citrate (SCA), Motility)
Macroscopic Colony Characterization (size, shape, margin, elevation, color, texture)

gallery.caption: Gram staining result - Gram-negative (pink) bacteria
Bacterial isolation on selective media plate
Slide culture technique for fungal colony examination
Biochemical tests — catalase, citrate, and motility assays
Macroscopic fungal colony characterization — color, texture, radial furrow

projects.title: Water Quality Analysis & Environmental Monitoring
projects.description: Conducted comprehensive water quality assessment including sample collection, preparation, and analysis of pH, TSS, BOD, and turbidity parameters.

details.overview: Conducted water quality assessment by collecting groundwater samples and analyzing key physical and chemical parameters, including Dissolved Oxygen (DO), Total Suspended Solids (TSS), pH, temperature, and turbidity. Performed laboratory measurements, data recording, result interpretation, and comparison with environmental quality standards.
details.challenge: Groundwater quality is influenced by multiple physical and chemical parameters. Accurate sample collection, instrument operation, and data interpretation were required to evaluate overall water quality and identify potential environmental concerns.
details.solution: Collected water samples following laboratory instructions, measured DO, TSS, pH, temperature, and turbidity using appropriate laboratory instruments, documented analytical results, and evaluated water quality based on established environmental standards.
results: Measured Dissolved Oxygen (DO) of well water at 4.75 mg/L, indicating dissolved oxygen below the recommended value for Class I water quality.
Calculated Total Suspended Solids (TSS) at 100 mg/L using the gravimetric method, meeting the reference range for raw drinking water.
Recorded water temperature of 30°C, indicating a relatively high temperature compared to the normal groundwater range.
Measured turbidity at 33 NTU, exceeding the Indonesian drinking water turbidity standard (25 NTU).
Determined water pH of 7, indicating neutral groundwater conditions.
Documented and interpreted laboratory findings by comparing measured values with environmental quality standards.

gallery.caption: Turbidity measurement using turbidimeter — 33 NTU
pH measurement — neutral groundwater at pH 7
Universal pH indicator color comparison

projects.title: Repin Aja-Relief Pintar Aksara Jawa
projects.description: REPIN AJA (Relief Pintar Aksara Jawa) is an inclusive educational innovation designed to help visually impaired students learn Javanese script through tactile relief characters, Braille, and audio guidance. The project integrates educational technology with cultural preservation to create an accessible and interactive learning experience.

details.overview: Contributed to the development of REPIN AJA (Relief Pintar Aksara Jawa), an inclusive educational learning media designed to help visually impaired students learn Javanese script through tactile relief characters, Braille, and audio-based interactions. Responsible for project planning, educational content development, implementation, user training, and program evaluation to enhance Javanese script literacy while supporting inclusive education and cultural preservation.
details.challenge: Visually impaired students have limited access to learning media for Javanese script, as conventional teaching materials rely heavily on visual instruction. This creates barriers to understanding and reduces opportunities to engage with local cultural education.
details.solution: Developed a multisensory learning medium that integrates embossed Javanese characters, Braille labels, and Arduino-powered audio guidance. The interactive design enables students to explore Javanese script independently through touch and sound, making the learning experience more accessible and engaging.
results: 92% of teachers at SLBA Yaketunis are able to teach Javanese script effectively using the Repin Aja media
88% of students are able to master all materials from level 1 to 6
Increased student interest in learning Javanese language

gallery.caption: Tactile relief Javanese characters with Braille labels
Arduino-powered audio guidance module integration
Language Translation Button (Indonesia and Javanese)
Power Buttton and Port Charger

projects.title: Attendance Tracker Dashboard
projects.description: Excel-based dashboard for tracking and analyzing employee attendance across multiple metrics.

details.overview: Developed an Excel-based Attendance Tracker Dashboard to automate attendance recording and summarize attendance data. Implemented formulas including VLOOKUP, IF, IFERROR, SUMIF, and INDEX-MATCH to process attendance records accurately. Created Pivot Tables and summary dashboards to monitor attendance trends, department performance, and individual attendance rates, reducing manual calculations and improving administrative efficiency.
details.challenge: Manual attendance tracking is time-consuming and prone to calculation errors, especially when managing records for multiple participants. Generating attendance summaries and department-level reports also required repetitive manual work.
details.solution: Designed an automated Excel workbook that validates attendance records, calculates attendance percentages, identifies attendance status, and generates dynamic summaries using formulas and Pivot Tables. The dashboard provides quick insights into department performance and individual attendance statistics.
results: Automated attendance percentage calculations using Excel formulas.
Generated department-wise attendance summaries with Pivot Tables.
Reduced manual data processing through formula-based automation.
Improved accuracy and consistency of attendance reporting.

gallery.caption: Attendance records with automated percentage calculation
Dashboard summary — department-wise attendance overview

projects.title: Sales Data Analysis
projects.description: Excel-based sales data analysis project that transformed raw datasets into structured reports using advanced formulas and Pivot Tables, demonstrating practical skills in data cleaning, reporting, and business analysis.

details.overview: Developed a sales data analysis dashboard as part of a self-directed Microsoft Excel learning project. Cleaned and standardized raw sales data using advanced text functions, calculated revenue metrics, and created Pivot Table reports to analyze sales performance across countries and continents. The project demonstrates practical skills in data transformation, reporting, and spreadsheet automation for business decision-making.
details.challenge: Raw sales data contained inconsistent text formatting and required extensive cleaning before meaningful analysis could be performed. Generating sales summaries across different regions also involved repetitive manual calculations.
details.solution: Applied Excel text functions, including TRIM, LEFT, MID, RIGHT, FIND, and SUBSTITUTE, to standardize the dataset. Calculated sales revenue using Excel formulas and created Pivot Tables to summarize sales performance by country and continent, enabling faster reporting and clearer business insights.
results: Standardized raw sales data for consistent analysis.
Automated revenue calculations using Excel formulas.
Generated Pivot Table summaries by country and continent.
Improved data consistency through text transformation functions.
Produced structured reports to support data-driven decision making.

gallery.caption: Raw data cleaning using TRIM, LEFT, MID, RIGHT functions
Pivot Table — sales performance by country
Revenue calculation and continental sales summary

projects.title: BioLearn — Interactive Biology Learning Platform
projects.description: Full-stack web application for interactive biology learning with chapter-based materials, flashcards, quizzes, praktikum viewer, progress tracking, and a comprehensive admin panel.

details.overview: Designed and developed BioLearn, a full-stack interactive biology learning platform built with Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, and Supabase. The platform features a structured chapter-based learning system (Bab → Sub-Bab) with rich content rendering, interactive flashcards with image hotspots, automated quiz system, bilingual praktikum viewer, real-time announcement bell, and per-student progress tracking. Includes a full admin dashboard for content management, user analytics, AI-assisted tools, and system configuration.
details.challenge: Traditional biology learning platforms rely on static PDF content with no interactive elements, making it difficult for students to engage with complex biological concepts. Managing structured educational content with chapters, sub-chapters, quizzes, and multimedia across different content types required a flexible and scalable architecture.
details.solution: Built a modular content architecture using Supabase as the backend with structured Bab/Sub-Bab hierarchy. Implemented rich content rendering with accordion sections, interactive flashcard system with image hotspot mapping, and a bilingual praktikum viewer. Used Zustand for client state management, Framer Motion for smooth transitions, and a media proxy layer for optimized image delivery. The admin panel provides full CRUD for all content types with real-time analytics.
results: Structured learning system with Bab → Sub-Bab hierarchy covering complete biology curriculum
Interactive flashcards with image hotspot mapping for visual learning
Automated quiz system with instant feedback and progress tracking
Bilingual praktikum viewer (Indonesian/English) with step-by-step guidance
Real-time announcement system with 60-second polling and read/unread tracking
Full admin dashboard with content management, user analytics, and AI-assisted tools
Password reset via email using Resend integration
Glossary system for biology terminology reference

gallery.caption: Student dashboard — chapter-based learning system with progress tracking
Admin panel — content management and analytics dashboard
Interactive learning materials
Interactive flash card with image hotspot mapping
